findings.description: Duplicate _conn contextmanager method — second definition shadows the first. Two @contextmanager def _conn(self) methods exist in _BatchStatusStorage class. First at line 550, second at line 560. The first is unreachable dead code.
Module-level _external_provider uses bare callable instead of Callable from typing. Should use Callable[[str, str], tuple[bool, str | None, str | None]] | None.
findings.evidence: _external_provider: callable | None = None (line 148) and def register_external_transcript_provider(provider: callable) (line 151) — both use bare callable

findings.description: Module uses Dict, List, Tuple, Optional from typing without from __future__ import annotations. Inconsistent with codebase style which uses from __future__ import annotations in most files.
findings.evidence: from typing import Optional, Dict, List, Tuple (line 25) — no from __future__ import annotations

findings.description: Unreachable code: break at line 299 exits inner while before else branch at line 300 can execute. The futures assignment is permanently unreachable.
findings.evidence: while futures loop: break exits before line 300 futures assignment. Comment at 301-302 describes intent but code can never reach there.

findings.description: Module-level mutable state _external_provider written by register_external_transcript_provider() with no thread-safety. Unpredictable behavior across imports or test ordering.
findings.evidence: global _external_provider; _external_provider = provider (line 164) — no threading.Lock despite module-level mutable state

findings.description: Bare except Exception: pass swallows all exceptions including ValueError that select_provider() should raise for invalid inputs. Docstring says it raises ValueError on invalid video_id format but this block silently converts those to routing decisions.
findings.evidence: try: from csf.batch_status import get_provider_scores; except Exception: pass (line 265)

findings.description: delete_channel sets deleted = True unconditionally before except block. Return value is always True regardless of whether delete succeeded.
findings.evidence: deleted = True (line 1011) fires even if commit() raises. except: conn.rollback(); raise. return deleted — always True.

findings.description: Returning manually-constructed subprocess.CompletedProcess with positional args. Style is confusing — explicit keyword args would be clearer.
findings.evidence: return subprocess.CompletedProcess(["nlm"] + args, 1, "", "Auth failed") — positional args make field mapping unclear

findings.description: close() method has no return type annotation. Inconsistent with other methods. Also _cleanup_staging_on_close() (line 1815) missing annotation.
Using lambda assigned to handler.emit bypasses proper inheritance pattern. Proper pattern is to subclass logging.Handler and override emit.
findings.evidence: handler.emit = lambda record: _queue_emit(handler, record, log_queue)  # type: ignore[assignment] (line 144)

findings.description: Using global _gemini_available inside method while _gemini_lock is used correctly at module level. Global declaration is misleading about concurrency semantics.
Bare except: clause catches BaseException including SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt. Should be except Exception: at minimum.
findings.evidence: try: sources = json.loads(res.stdout); except: return {vid: (False, None, "Parse failed") for vid in batch_ids} (line 703)

summary: Found 12 issues across 8 files. Critical: batch_status.py duplicate _conn contextmanager (unreachable code); batch.py line 300 unreachable; nlm_batch.py line 703 bare except catching SystemExit. Medium: orchestrator.py line 265 bare except swallowing errors; transcript.py line 148 bare callable type; delete_channel always returns True. Minor: nlm_scraper.py typing imports inconsistent; nlm_scraper.py close() missing annotation; csf_logging.py lambda emit pattern.
